task:
Implement the CodeRabbit Coding Plan for issue #2578: Brain Dump to Outline - address CodeRabbit feedback and re-promote ## Instructions 1. `cd ~/2025slideheroes-sophie` 2. `git fetch upstream && git push origin upstream/dev:dev --force` 3. `git checkout upstream/dev && git checkout -b sophie/issue-2578` 4. Read the full issue: `gh issue view 2578 --repo slideheroes/2025slideheroes` 5. Implement according to the Coding Plan below 6. Run `pnpm format:fix && pnpm lint:fix && pnpm typecheck` before committing 7. Commit with: `git commit -m "feat: Brain Dump to Outline - address CodeRabbit feedback and re-promote (#2578)"` 8. Push: `git push origin sophie/issue-2578` 9. Open PR on the **fork** for CodeRabbit review (NOT on upstream): ```bash gh pr create --repo slideheroes/2025slideheroes-sophie \ --base dev \ --head sophie/issue-2578 \ --title "feat: Brain Dump to Outline - address CodeRabbit feedback and re-promote" \ --body "Implements slideheroes/2025slideheroes#2578 Implemented per CodeRabbit Coding Plan. > This PR is on the fork for CodeRabbit review. It will be promoted to upstream automatically once CR is satisfied and CI passes." ``` **IMPORTANT:** - Open the PR on the FORK (`slideheroes/2025slideheroes-sophie`), NOT on upstream (`slideheroes/2025slideheroes`). - The PR will be automatically promoted to upstream after CodeRabbit review. - Do NOT open a PR on upstream directly. ## CodeRabbit Coding Plan ## Coding Plan ### Summary - Centralize and tighten Zod schemas to catch validation issues early rather than letting malformed data reach the database or produce broken outlines - Add explicit JSON output instructions to prompts so LLM responses can be reliably parsed, matching the pattern established in other generation templates - Gate step advancement behind an explicit flag to prevent auto-save from prematurely advancing workflow state - Expose prompt assets from the ai-gateway barrel so consumers can import them directly rather than relying on internal paths ## Implementation Steps ### Phase 1: Tighten Schema Validation Centralize the action input schema and add missing constraints to prevent empty or malformed data from passing validation silently.
Task 1: Centralize GenerateOutlineSchema Move the locally-defined schema to the centralized schema file and update imports. - Add `GenerateOutlineSchema` to `apps/web/app/home/(user)/ai/_lib/schemas/presentation-artifacts.ts` after the `OutlineContentSchema` block (around line 212) - Schema should validate `presentationId` as `z.string().uuid()` instead of `z.string().min(1)` to catch malformed IDs before DB queries - Update `apps/web/app/home/(user)/ai/[id]/outline/_actions/generate-outline.action.ts` to import from the centralized location and remove the local definition at lines 151-154
Task 2: Add Minimum Constraints to LLM Response Schemas Prevent the LLM from returning empty sections that pass validation but produce broken outlines. - In `presentation-artifacts.ts`, update `LLMSectionSchema.title` and `LLMSectionSchema.content` from `z.string()` to `z.string().min(1)` - Update `LLMOutlineResponseSchema.sections` from `z.array(LLMSectionSchema)` to `z.array(LLMSectionSchema).min(1)` to ensure at least one section
Task 3: Fix Zod Error Handling in Generate Action The current error logging uses an incorrect type cast that masks proper typing. - In `generate-outline.action.ts` lines 508-515, replace `(validationResult.error as { errors?: unknown }).errors` with `validationResult.error.issues` or `validationResult.error.flatten()` - This removes the unsafe cast and provides properly typed error information
🤖 Prompt for AI agents ``` Tighten Zod schema validation across the outline feature — the goal is to stop malformed/empty data from silently passing validation and reaching the DB or producing broken outlines. **Task 1: Centralize `GenerateOutlineSchema`** - In `apps/web/app/home/(user)/ai/_lib/schemas/presentation-artifacts.ts`, add `GenerateOutlineSchema` after the `OutlineContentSchema` block (~line 212) - `presentationId` must use `z.string().uuid()` — not `z.string().min(1)` — to catch malformed IDs before any DB query runs - In `apps/web/app/home/(user)/ai/[id]/outline/_actions/generate-outline.action.ts`, remove the local schema definition at lines 151-154 and import `GenerateOutlineSchema` from the centralized schema file **Task 2: Add minimum constraints to LLM response schemas** - In `presentation-artifacts.ts`, update `LLMSectionSchema`: change `title` and `content` from `z.string()` to `z.string().min(1)` - Update `LLMOutlineResponseSchema.sections` from `z.array(LLMSectionSchema)` to `z.array(LLMSectionSchema).min(1)` — an empty sections array should be a validation failure, not a silent success **Task 3: Fix unsafe type cast in error handling** - In `generate-outline.action.ts` lines 508-515, replace the unsafe cast `(validationResult.error as { errors?: unknown }).errors` with `validationResult.error.issues` or `validationResult.error.flatten()` to get properly typed Zod error information ```
### Phase 2: Fix JSON Prompt Instructions The test-outline prompts request plain text output but consumers expect JSON. Add explicit JSON-only instructions matching `LLMOutlineResponseSchema`.
Task 1: Update System Prompt with JSON Format Add output format instructions to the system message. - In `packages/ai-gateway/src/prompts/messages/system/test-outline-creator.ts`, append a JSON output block after the guidelines section (around line 22) - Instruction should specify: respond ONLY with valid JSON, no markdown fences, no prose, use exact structure `{"sections": [{"title": "...", "content": "..."}]}`
Task 2: Update User Prompt with JSON Requirements Replace the free-form output expectation with explicit JSON structure. - In `packages/ai-gateway/src/prompts/messages/user/test-outline-request.ts`, add at the end (before line 47) explicit JSON output directive - Match the pattern used in `situation-improvements/index.ts` lines 42-64 which embeds the exact JSON schema expected
🤖 Prompt for AI agents ``` The test-outline prompts are broken — they ask for plain text but the consumers parse JSON. Fix both the system and user prompts to enforce strict JSON output matching `LLMOutlineResponseSchema`. **Task 1: System prompt — `test-outline-creator.ts`** - File: `packages/ai-gateway/src/prompts/messages/system/test-outline-creator.ts` - After the guidelines section (~line 22), append a JSON output block - The instruction must state: respond ONLY with valid JSON, no markdown fences, no prose, using the exact structure `{"sections": [{"title": "...", "content": "..."}]}` **Task 2: User prompt — `test-outline-request.ts`** - File: `packages/ai-gateway/src/prompts/messages/user/test-outline-request.ts` - Before line 47, add an explicit JSON output directive - Follow the pattern from `situation-improvements/index.ts` lines 42-64, which embeds the exact expected JSON schema inline ```
### Phase 3: Fix Action Logic Bug ## Coding Rules # Coding Rules These rules document the actual code quality patterns used in the SlideHeroes codebase. ## TypeScript Patterns - Strict mode compliance: enable all strict TypeScript options - Use explicit local type aliases rather than inline complex types - Use `z.infer` for Zod-derived types - Use `instanceof Error` narrowing in catch blocks instead of `instanceof` checks on non-Error values - Never use `any` - use `unknown` with type guards when needed ## API Route Patterns - Use Next.js 15 style with `params` as Promise - Use named exports (GET, POST, PATCH, DELETE) for route handlers - Use `.catch(() => ({}))` for body parsing error handling - Use `NextResponse.json({ error })` response shape - Validate input at boundaries using Zod schemas ## Prisma Patterns - Import Prisma client from `@/lib/db` - Use conditional spread for PATCH updates (e.g., `...(data || {})`) - Use `.catch(() => null)` for not-found handling ## Import Conventions - Use `@/` alias for src imports (e.g., `@/components/Button`) - Import from specific files, avoid barrel exports - Separate client and server imports - never mix them from the same file ## Error Handling - Use tight try/catch around specific I/O operations only - Use `logger.error({ err }, '...')` for structured logging - Import logger from `@kit/shared/logger` - Handle errors gracefully using try/catch and appropriate error types - Use `isRedirectError` from `next/dist/client/components/redirect-error` for client-side redirect handling ## React Patterns - Encapsulate repeated blocks of code into reusable local components - Write small, composable, explicit, well-named components - Always use `react-hook-form` and `@kit/ui/form` for forms - Use client components only when needed for interactivity (use `use client` directive) - Add `data-testid` for E2E tests where appropriate - Prefer server-side data fetching using RSC - Display loading indicators where appropriate ## Next.js Patterns - Use `enhanceAction` for Server Actions from `@kit/next/actions` - Use `use server` in server action files - Export page components using the `withI18n` utility - Add well-written page metadata to pages - Redirect using `redirect` following a server action instead of using client-side router ## Data Fetching Patterns - Use async server components that call loader functions for initial data fetching - Use `enhanceAction` for create/update/delete operations with schema validation - Use `Promise.all()` for parallel data fetching in Server Components ## Linting and Formatting Before committing, always run: ```bash pnpm format:fix && pnpm lint:fix && pnpm typecheck ``` ## Component Organization - Route-specific components: `_components/` directories - Route utilities: `_lib/` for client, `_lib/server/` for server-side - Global components: Root-level directories ## Naming Conventions - Pages: `page.tsx` (Next.js convention) - Loaders: `{feature}-page.loader.ts` - Actions: `{feature}-server-actions.ts` - Schemas: `{feature}.schema.ts` - Components: `kebab-case.tsx`
